rawContent.message: No previous snapshot found — first QA run, full checks performed

build.notes: Build compiles successfully. Lint errors exist in unrelated files (MainWrapper/index.tsx: prettier + rules-of-hooks; FullWidthMedia.tsx: no-base-control-with-label-without-id; TestimonialsCarousel.tsx: no-nested-ternary). No lint errors in Header block files.

registration.notes: block.json schema valid, naming correct (takt/header), all file references exist, child blocks registered, attribute types valid.

tsxPhpSync.notes: taktician_validate_all sync check passed. No PHP extract anti-pattern, docblocks present with @var annotations, HTML structure in sync, attribute usage consistent. TSX HeaderMainItem editor uses md:block on submenu when open; PHP uses hidden/block toggle via JS — structurally equivalent.

attributes.notes: All block.json attributes correctly typed. fixedOnScrollDesktop, fixedOnScrollMobile, hideOnScrollDownDesktop, hideOnScrollDownMobile are booleans with false defaults. logoId is optional integer. sepLink, searchLink, mobileCtaLink are objects. mobileCtaText is a string. Data attributes on rendered HTML correctly reflect attribute values (verified via page_audit data-fixed-scroll-desktop, data-hide-scroll-down-desktop etc.).

edgeCases.notes: Minimal content (no submenus, no SEP link, no CTA) renders cleanly. Not-fixed variant (section 4) has no z-index classes — correct. SEP-link-only variant renders correctly. Many nav items (8) variant renders without layout errors.

testsRun.notes: Clicking .header-main-item-button opens submenu; aria-expanded set to 'true', submenu gains 'block' class, loses 'hidden'.

testsRun.test: Click outside (within header nav area) closes dropdown
testsRun.notes: Clicking .header-main nav element (outside submenu panel and outside trigger button) closes the open submenu. JS document click handler correctly checks clickedInsideSubmenu and clickedTrigger before closing.

testsRun.test: Click on another top-level button closes prior dropdown
testsRun.notes: On desktop, click handler calls closeAllSubmenus() for all other buttons before opening the clicked one.

testsRun.test: ArrowDown on trigger opens submenu and focuses first item
testsRun.notes: ArrowDown keydown on .header-main-item-button opens submenu and focuses items[0].
Mobile (375px) menu toggle button present. aria-expanded correctly set. menu-open class toggles header element.

patternCompliance.notes: No taktician_a11y_test rule file exists for 'menubar' pattern — manual review performed.
passed: ArrowDown on trigger button opens submenu and moves focus to first menuitem
ArrowUp on trigger button opens submenu and moves focus to last menuitem
ArrowDown/Up within open submenu cycles through menuitems
Home/End within open submenu jump to first/last menuitem
Escape within submenu closes it and returns focus to trigger button
Tab while submenu open closes submenu (line 387)
ArrowLeft/Right within submenu closes current and moves focus to prev/next top-level button
ArrowLeft/Right on trigger button moves focus to prev/next top-level button (with submenu close if open)
Home/End on trigger button moves focus to first/last top-level button
failed: Left/Right arrow navigation on top-level buttons ONLY covers buttons with aria-haspopup (subMenuButtons array). Simple link items (no submenu) are excluded from Left/Right keyboard traversal. In the full-content header, 'Student Voices' is a plain link and is skipped when using Left/Right arrows from adjacent submenu buttons.
role='menubar' is absent on the top-level <ul>. WAI-ARIA menubar pattern requires role='menubar' on the containing list element and role='menuitem' on top-level interactive items.

markupDuplication.notes: Search form appears twice: once in the desktop utility row (hidden md:flex) and once in the mobile collapsible panel (md:hidden). This is an intentional layout pattern — the two forms serve genuinely different layout contexts (utility row vs. collapsible mobile panel). No JS cloning. Acceptable per duplication rules.

innerBlocks.notes: Inner blocks structure correct: takt/header > takt/header-main > takt/header-main-item > takt/header-main-sub-item. Template lock not applied (items can be added/removed as expected). 9 header instances confirmed across 9 demo containers.

specCompliance.notes: All 5 focus areas from the planning spec addressed in code. 9 block instances present on test page in demo containers. No stale HTML markup. No placeholder media IDs.

issues.description: Left/Right arrow keyboard navigation on the menubar is limited to buttons with submenus (subMenuButtons array, built from .header-main-item-button[aria-haspopup='true']). Plain link items (header-main-items without a submenu) are invisible to Left/Right traversal. In the full-content header 'Student Voices' is a plain link and is skipped — keyboard users cannot navigate to or from it using arrow keys, they must use Tab instead, breaking the WAI-ARIA menubar pattern.
issues.fix: Expand the top-level items array to include ALL focusable top-level items (both .header-main-item-button and plain .header-main-item > a[role!='menuitem']). Build a separate allTopLevelItems array: const allTopLevelItems = Array.from(header.querySelectorAll('.header-main-item > button.header-main-item-button, .header-main-item > a')); Use allTopLevelItems for Left/Right/Home/End navigation at the menubar level. Keep subMenuButtons only for submenu open/close logic.

issues.description: The top-level navigation <ul> element (rendered as <ul class='flex md:flex-row ...'>) has no role='menubar'. The WAI-ARIA Navigation Menubar pattern requires role='menubar' on the containing list. Without it, assistive technologies do not announce the menubar keyboard interaction model to users, defeating the purpose of implementing the WAI-ARIA pattern.
issues.fix: Add role='menubar' to the top-level <ul> element in HeaderMain.php (line 150) and the corresponding <ul {...innerBlocksProps} /> in HeaderMain.tsx (line 733). The attribute must also be added at render: in PHP: <ul role="menubar" class="...">; in TSX: pass role:'menubar' into the innerBlocksProps object or add it directly to the <ul> element.

issues.description: Individual top-level nav items do not have role='menuitem'. The WAI-ARIA menubar pattern expects role='menuitem' on each interactive item within the menubar (both trigger buttons and plain links). Currently the buttons have aria-haspopup but no explicit role, and the plain links have no role. This is a minor gap — browsers may infer roles, but explicit roles improve AT interoperability.
issues.fix: Add role='menuitem' to both the <button> element (line 35-46) and the ThemeLink-rendered <a> element (via the 'className' or a dedicated role parameter) in HeaderMainItem.php. Mirror in HeaderMainItem.tsx button (line 127) and Link component (line 107).

passedChecks: Build compiles successfully — no Header block TypeScript or compilation errors
Block registration valid (takt/header, takt/header-main, takt/header-main-item, takt/header-main-sub-item)
Fix 1 VERIFIED: Fixed header constrained to container width using left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 w-(--max-container) when scrolling — confirmed in rendered class list for all fixed-scroll variants
Fix 2 VERIFIED: z-index md:z-200 present on header-main element for all fixed-scroll variants; submenus carry md:z-[210] class — confirmed via page_audit extraSelectors

Fix 3 VERIFIED: Dropdown gap is md:mt-6 (24px) between header bar and panel top — confirmed in HeaderMainItem.php line 53; hover bridge pseudo-element present (before:md:absolute before:md:bottom-full before:md:h-6) — confirmed via page_audit hover_bridge selector found on all submenu-bearing instances

Fix 4 VERIFIED: Click-outside handler checks clickedInsideSubmenu AND clickedTrigger separately (HeaderMain.js lines 482-484), closing dropdown when clicking anywhere in the nav outside the panel and its trigger — confirmed via interactive screenshot test

Fix 5 PARTIAL: ArrowDown/Up open submenu and navigate within it; ArrowLeft/Right within submenu close and move to adjacent button; Home/End within submenu navigate to first/last item; Escape closes and returns focus; Tab while submenu open closes it — all working. See FQA-001/FQA-002 for incomplete aspects.
TSX/PHP sync passes all automated checks
PHP template uses auto-extracted variables (no $attributes['key'] anti-pattern)
All @var docblock annotations present for camelCase attributes
Dropdown submenu z-index z-[210] confirmed on all submenu instances (page_audit extraSelectors)
Hover bridge pseudo-element (before:md:h-6) confirmed on all submenu instances
Test page has 9 header block instances, all inside demo containers
No placeholder media IDs (logoId:6 is a real image ID across all instances)
Markup duplication (dual search forms) is intentional and correctly structured
Mobile menu toggle with aria-expanded and focus trapping implemented correctly
